items.title: The Dort deal, Summer League takeaways, early draft preview + my review of 'The Odyssey' with Es Baraheni & Isaac Trotter
items.summary: Subscribe to The Kevin O'Connor ShowApple Podcasts | Spotify | YouTubeOn today’s Kevin O’Connor Show, KOC gives his thoughts on the Oklahoma City Thunder trading Lu Dort. Was this really a financial decision or is that just an excuse for OKC to part ways with Dort? Also, will LeBron make his decision soon? And does anyone really know where he will play next season?Then, Kevin is joined by CBS Sports’ Isaac Trotter to talk Las Vegas Summer League and some of the biggest names to watch in the 2027 and 2028 NBA draft classes.Plus, The Athletic’s Es Baraheni joins to rave about Allen Graves and Christopher Nolan’s latest film, "The Odyssey."(0:37) Thunder trade Lu Dort to Atlanta(8:17) Latest on LeBron James(14:22) Golden State wins Summer League(21:43) Grizzlies' bright future(25:11) Brayden Burries & Keaton Wagler Summer League recap(29:04) 2nd year Summer League…

items.title: NBA Offseason Trade/Free Agent Rumors 2026: Lakers, Hawks not close to Kuminga deal, Jalen Duren latest
items.summary: While the NBA continues to wait for LeBron James to announce where he's going to play next season — and there is NBA business held up because of it — there are still other talks and things going on. Here are some of the latest non-LeBron rumors.Lakers, Hawks not close on Jonathan KumingaThere is a segment of Lakers fans talking them into Jonathan Kuminga as a difference-maker, likely the same fans who talked themselves into Deandre Ayton being a difference-maker a year ago despite a long history suggesting otherwise (which echoes Kuminga).That may not be an issue because the Lakers and Hawks are nowhere close to a deal, reports Dan Woike at The Athletic."...it appears that momentum has stalled with the sides far apart on terms and L.A. not overly motivated to push significant draft capital into sign-and-trade scenarios. Still, he appears to be the biggest upside play left on…

items.title: LeBron James' delayed decision is becoming a horrible look | Opinion
items.summary: Today better be the day.We’ve waited long enough.LeBron James has kept the NBA and its fans twisting in the wind. Dragging out an announcement of where he’ll continue his illustrious basketball career.In some ways, it's too late to reverse the reputational damage.James, at 41, still has the ability to play like a man half his age. Now he’s acting like a man half his age as if desperate for attention. If he chooses any team but the Cleveland Cavaliers, he risks alienating the fans in a city where he’s considered the prodigal son. Regardless, the process already has dredged up memories of “The Decision.’’That decision took place in 2010. A one-hour special hosted by Jim Gray that aired on ESPN. During "The Decision,'' James announced, "I'm going to take my talents to South Beach and join the Miami Heat.’’Now it's James' failure to say where he's taking his talents that's created…

items.title: How Atlanta has benefited from OKC's quest to remain a legit contender
items.summary: When the Oklahoma City Thunder shipped Lu Dort — and previously Aaron Wiggins — to the Atlanta Hawks, it signaled a change in direction for both franchises.For the Thunder, they were cost-saving moves that signaled a pivot to a more tempered roster construction philosophy, where dollars and cents matter more and more, as their payroll is about to skyrocket.For the Hawks, they were aggressive moves, signaling their willingness to act as buyers to make a deep postseason push in the Eastern Conference.Dort, a defensive specialist, is replacing former top pick Zaccharie Risacher on Atlanta's depth chart, as the French prospect is bound for Dallas in a three-team trade. The transaction is a strong indicator that the Hawks are trying to elevate to compete sooner rather than later.Said differently: The Hawks are not messing around right now and are willing to take on money and relinquish…

items.title: Fantasy Basketball Rankings: Top-40 rookies for dynasty formats following Las Vegas Summer League
items.summary: The NBA Summer League wrapped up in Las Vegas on Sunday with the Golden State Warriors taking home the championship, and rookie Yaxel Lendenborg winning MVP honors. While it's always dangerous to overreact to a handful of games against inconsistent competition, it gave us our first real look at how this rookie class translates to the NBA level.This top 40 is built for 10- and 12-team leagues running three-round rookie drafts (36 picks), with a handful of extra names thrown in for depth. It's a deep class once again and there will certainly be some surprises — especially at the top of the board. Let's get to it.Rookie rankings: The top 101. Caleb Wilson - F/C,  Chicago BullsWe're coming out swinging. Wilson over the consensus first pick, Cameron Boozer. I think Wilson has the highest ceiling in the class. Scoring, rebounding, stocks, 3s and a dog mentality — he looks…
